Protein Folding Dynamics in Aqueous Solutions

Abstract

We investigate the folding dynamics of small globular proteins using molecular
dynamics simulations. Our results reveal previously uncharacterized intermediate
states in the folding pathway of ubiquitin.

1 Introduction

Protein folding remains one of the grand challenges in molecular biology. Despite
recent advances with AlphaFold2, the kinetic pathways of folding are not fully
understood.

2 Methods

All-atom molecular dynamics simulations were performed using GROMACS 2023.
The CHARMM36m force field was employed with TIP3P water model. Simulations
were run for 10 microseconds each at 300K and 350K.

3 Results

Three distinct intermediate states were identified along the folding pathway.
The first intermediate (I1) forms within 500 nanoseconds and involves
partial formation of the alpha helix. See Figure 1 for the energy landscape.

4 Discussion

Our findings suggest a hierarchical folding mechanism consistent with the
framework model. The intermediate states observed here may be relevant
to understanding misfolding diseases.

5 Conclusion

We have characterized the folding pathway of ubiquitin with unprecedented
temporal resolution. These results provide a foundation for rational drug design
targeting folding intermediates.

Acknowledgments

This work was supported by NIH grant R01-GM123456. Computational resources
were provided by XSEDE allocation BIO210001.

References

[1] Jumper, J. et al. (2021). Nature, 596, 583-589.
[2] Shaw, D.E. et al. (2010). Science, 330, 341-346.

Appendix A: Simulation Parameters

Force field: CHARMM36m
Water model: TIP3P
Box size: 7.0 nm cubic
Ion concentration: 150 mM NaCl
Temperature: 300K, 350K
Pressure: 1 bar (Parrinello-Rahman)
Time step: 2 fs
PME cutoff: 1.2 nm

Appendix B: Additional Figures

Table S1: RMSD values for all simulation replicas
Replica  Temp(K)  RMSD_avg(nm)  RMSD_std(nm)  Frames
1        300      0.234         0.045         50000
2        300      0.251         0.052         50000
3        300      0.228         0.041         50000
4        350      0.312         0.067         50000
5        350      0.298         0.058         50000

Supporting Information

Additional analysis including contact maps, secondary structure timelines,
and free energy surfaces are available in the online Supporting Information.
Hydrogen bond analysis and solvent accessible surface area calculations
are also provided.
